entry.title: SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management Localization: Innovations in 2502
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community!

Welcome back to the first blog post for the year 2025! This time, we bring you the latest innovations and features in localization of SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502

Here we are talking about a comprehensive solution that handles subscription and usage-based models. It provides a standardized approach to recurring billing, invoicing, tax compliance, and automation.

With each release, SAP introduces localized country/region versions and new functions to assist customers with tax compliance, billing, invoicing, automation, and streamlined processes. This not only helps reduce compliance risks and costs but also improves the sustainability of tax operations.

For example, in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502, several countries/regions and functions were delivered in the product area of SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management for localization.

Additional functions, which were not part of the roadmap, have also been delivered:

One example, from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 innovations, is the electronic submission of convergent invoices for Golden Tax invoice in China.

Electronic submission of convergent invoices for Golden Tax invoice in China

This video shows you how to submit invoices from convergent invoicing electronically using the integration of the golden tax interface.

 

For more information, I invite you to visit the following pages: 

  • Road Map Explorer: Future innovations planned 
  • SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management, public edition – Online Help Documentation
  • What’s New: What's New Viewer - SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Blog Post: SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management Localization: Sustainable Compliance for Your Recurring Revenue Business

The purpose of this post is to show you how SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management for SAP S/4HANA localization is a game-changer for businesses that are looking to streamline their financial operations while ensuring compliance with local regulations. Stay tuned for the next update!

I hope you’ve found this blog post interesting. If so, I invite you to follow my profile and the tags SAP Document and Reporting Compliance and SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management. Also, feel free to leave a comment or a suggestion in the comment section.

Thank you for choosing SAP to run your business complaint with local tax authorities!

entry.title: Exploring SAP's Regional Collaboration Groups on Localization Topics
entry.content:
SAP offers several channels for communication with its customers and partners to ensure effective support, feedback, and collaboration.  

The primary resource for it is the SAP Support Portal, where customers and partners can access support, product documentation, knowledge base articles, and use various self-service options.  

For direct communication on localization topics there are dozens of digital regional collaboration groups, or workspaces, managed by Local Product Managers (LPMs) of SAP. Each group is a network of SAP users, industry experts, and technology professionals, where they share ideas, knowledge, and experiences related to the localization in a certain region and country. All groups are divided by their product and solution category: ERP, HCM and Payroll, and SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. 

Members of the groups will be able to find fresh and useful materials on localization in their region: FAQs, learning material, links to numerous relevant resources and interesting blog posts. Additionally, you can discover links to:

- Regulatory Change Manager (RCM)

 - Localization community of SAP

-  SAP's localization event calendar

One of the essential functionalities of the workspaces is the Forum. It is an online discussion platform where members can engage in conversations, ask questions, share information, and collaborate. It is remarkable that all groups are closed and require authentication for access.

To engage with SAP users and experts on topics related to localization and upcoming regulatory changes in your country, please find here the workspace that aligns with your interests.

Do you want to stay informed about the upcoming events? Check out the SAP's localization event calendar.

Feel free to ask any questions about the local collaboration groups you may have in the comments section below.

entry.title: Local Roots, Global Impact: How Grupo Nabeiro Navigated the Business Landscape in Angola
entry.content:
As we continue our journey through a dynamic and ever-changing global landscape, we're thrilled to bring you the next captivating story in our ongoing blog series, featuring remarkable tales of innovation and resilience. 
In this exciting installment, we spotlight the incredible journey of Grupo Nabeiro, a Portugal-based coffee powerhouse, and their impressive strides in the complex market of Angola. 

Grupo Nabeiro: A Journey of Aromatic Transformation and Resilient Growth

In the aromatic and culturally rich world of coffee, stories of growth, triumph, and innovation abound. Among these, the journey of Portugal-based Grupo Nabeiro stands out as a remarkable tale of perseverance and transformation. Celebrating over 61 years of operation, the company, founded by Manuel Rui Azinhais Nabeiro, affectionately known as Mr. Rui Nabeiro, has woven an inspiring narrative of overcoming challenges in one of the world's most complex yet rewarding markets – Angola.

Grupo Nabeiro’s journey, which began in 1961 with a small coffee startup in Campo Maior, Portugal, has now expanded its footprints across eight countries. This transformation from a local coffee roaster to an international powerhouse is nothing short of extraordinary. Mr. Nabeiro’s vision was not simply about business but about touching lives and empowering communities through the universal language of coffee.

This commitment to transformation and resilience resonated profoundly with Mr. Jose Beato, the Managing Director of Africa and Latin America for Grupo Nabeiro. During our conversation, Mr. Beato shared insightful stories about Grupo Nabeiro's remarkable transformation journey, particularly their significant ventures in Angola and managing local regulatory compliance.

Navigating Business Complexities Through Localization

One of Mr. Nabeiro's most significant business moves was the company's successful entry into Angola. Unlike any previous logistical endeavours, entering the Angolan market was no simple feat. The company had to adapt to a less developed business environment while complying with local regulations.

Successfully entering the Angolan market required Grupo Nabeiro to overcome various operational challenges, particularly in meeting local tax and regulatory requirements. Under Mr. Nabeiro's leadership, the company embarked on a mission to address these challenges without hindering business operations.

In 2019, the implementation of the Value-Added Tax (VAT) system in Angola marked a significant shift in the local economy. Recognizing the need for robust digital solutions, Grupo Nabeiro adopted SAP to streamline its operations and ensure compliance with the new tax regulations. This technological integration proved beneficial throughout their growth journey, allowing them to manage their business processes efficiently even in a complex environment.

An important achievement and testament to their preparedness and quick adaptation was successfully implementing SAF-T (Standard Audit File for Tax) compliance, a measure adopted by the Angolan authorities. By leveraging capabilities of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Grupo Nabeiro not only championed this critical transition smoothly but also led the process with local authorities, ensuring that they remained at the forefront of digital compliance.

The implementation of SAP facilitated the company's efficient handling of regulatory requirements and operational challenges, allowing it to focus on its mission of growth and sustainability in Angola. The benefits of localization through SAP enabled Grupo Nabeiro to remain agile and responsive in a complex market, ensuring their long-term success and commitment to the local economy.

Sustainable Business Through Localized Efforts

Grupo Nabeiro’s presence in Angola is grounded in environmental and social sustainability. The company began buying coffee from local farmers, offering fair prices and restoring an industry devastated by a 40-year war.

"We decided to buy coffee from local farmers, offering fair prices, and to reestablish the virtually extinct coffee industry," notes Mr. Beato. "We began purchasing coffee from local producers step by step, visiting areas that had not seen buyers in decades."

By 2000, Grupo Nabeiro started selling Delta coffee in Angola, and by 2001, they began industrial roasting operations of Ginga coffee. Ginga, named after an ancient queen renowned for her resistance against European colonization, had deep cultural significance and symbolized resilience to the Angolan people.

Mr. Beato’s decade-long experience in Angola was transformative. He forged profound connections with local culture and people through the coffee revival efforts. "My life experience there was profound," recounts Mr. Beato. " I will be a little bit always African for my life."

Grupo Nabeiro's localized approach has brought sustainable change, aiding local farmers and revitalizing small-scale coffee farming in remote areas lacking infrastructure.

Summary

Grupo Nabeiro’s journey from a humble coffee venture in Portugal to an international powerhouse underscores the importance of localization. By leveraging SAP’s localized capabilities, Grupo Nabeiro successfully navigated Angola’s complex regulatory environment and ensured sustainable business growth. Their commitment to supporting local farmers and adhering to local regulations highlights the power of a strong entrepreneurial spirit and a deep understanding of local markets.

As Grupo Nabeiro continues to transform its business and explore new territories, their journey in Angola serves as a testament to the values and benefits of localization, demonstrating the importance of adapting to and understanding local markets while fostering sustainable growth.

entry.title: SAP Document Reporting Compliance for Mauritius & Jordan
entry.content:
Mauritius: Simplifying Tax Reporting

Mauritius has a comprehensive tax system that requires businesses to file various reports, including VAT returns, corporate tax returns, and recently eInvoicing through the Mauritius Revenue Authority (MRA). With DRC, companies can streamline the entire reporting process by automating data collection, validation, submission and the “final mile” to the customer.

Key Benefits of DRC in Mauritius:

  • Automated eInvoice Generation: The solution generates eInvoices in the required formats, eliminating manual effort and reducing the risk of errors.
  • Audit Trail and Compliance Monitoring: DRC maintains a complete audit trail, enabling companies to track changes and demonstrate compliance during audits.
  • Localized Content: Through SAP DRC Extensibility we create local content packages for Mauritius, ensuring that you comply with the latest regulatory requirements.

Jordan: Enhancing Tax Transparency

Jordan has implemented various tax reforms in recent years, emphasizing transparency and accurate reporting. DRC can help businesses in Jordan meet these stringent requirements while minimizing the administrative burden as the Jordanian government rolls out its eInvoicing system.

Key Benefits of DRC in Jordan:

  • Electronic Submission: The solution supports electronic submission of tax returns, streamlining the submission process and reducing paper-based documentation.
  • Scalability: DRC can handle large volumes of data, making it suitable for businesses of all sizes operating in Jordan.

By implementing SAP DRC, companies in Mauritius and Jordan can significantly reduce the time and effort required for tax compliance while minimizing the risk of penalties and fines.

Our DRC Extensibility Template allows us to customize the functions and features as you require for your business scenario’s:

Mauritius: Live across ECC, S/4HANA, S/4 Private Cloud

  • Out of the box iFlow to the MRA
  • Flexible AIF Tables to map your data fields
  • Unique IRN Number stored in SAP
  • QR Code
  • Standard DRC Configuration

Utilizing our DRC template means your business has a solution already being used by multiple clients in Mauritius which to users is no different to any other DRC Country solution provided by SAP:

Jordan: Live across ECC, S/4HANA & S/4 Private Cloud (Live since the pilot began 2 years ago)

  • Out of the box iFlow to ISTD
  • Flexible AIF Tables
  • QR Code
  • Standard DRC Configuration

As with all DRC solutions all files are stored directly in DRC for users:

While DRC offers numerous benefits, implementing and configuring the solution can be a complex process, especially for businesses with unique requirements or operating in multiple jurisdictions. This is where CrimsonSAP can provide invaluable assistance.

We have a team of experienced consultants who specialize in DRC implementations and customization. We are currently helping multiple organisations on their eInvoicing journey in Mauritius and Jordan to navigate the complexities of the solution, ensuring a smooth and efficient deployment process.

  • Analysis: Our consultants will work closely with your team to understand your specific eInvoicing needs and regulatory requirements and customize our DRC solution to your organization.
  • Solution Design and Configuration: Based on the analysis, they will install our DRC extensibility template and configure DRC to meet your business requirements, including customizations and integrations with other systems.
  • User Training and Support: The team will provide knowledge transfer and training, ensuring you can effectively use and maintain the DRC extensible solution.
  • Ongoing Maintenance and Updates: As tax regulations evolve, we will help you stay up-to-date with the latest changes and ensure your DRC solution remains compliant.
  • Client success stories on linkedin coming soon

With our expertise and localized content, our DRC extensibility template empowers businesses to focus on their core operations while ensuring seamless eInvoicing integration with the tax authority and importantly your customers.

For multinational organizations already using SAP's Document Reporting Compliance (DRC) solution, Crimson offers a powerful way to extend and customize the platform to meet their unique global reporting needs.

In our next blogs we will explore the Baltics, Balkans, CIS and our experience in Malaysia and other full countries upcoming.

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Singapore GST InvoiceNow
entry.content:
GST InvoiceNow - submission of invoice data to Singapore Tax Authority (IRAS)

Image from IRAS GST InvoiceNow page

On April 15, 2024, The Inland Revenue Authority of Singapore (IRAS) published media release "Implementation of InvoiceNow for GST-Registered Businesses and free InvoiceNow Services for Newly In..." in the Newsroom. GST-registered businesses will be required to use InvoiceNow solutions to transmit the invoice data directly to IRAS for tax administration. By transmitting invoice data directly to IRAS via the InvoiceNow solutions, GST- registered businesses can streamline their compliance processes, reduce data preparation efforts for their submissions to IRAS and enjoy faster GST refunds.

GST InvoiceNow requirement would be implemented in phases, starting with soft launch from May 2025, allowing any existing GST-registered businesses that wish to be early adopters to transmit invoice data to IRAS using InvoiceNow solutions via InvoiceNow network.

  • From 1 May 2025, for GST-registered businesses that wish to be early adopters.
  • From 1 November 2025, for newly incorporated companies[1] that voluntarily apply for GST registration.
  • From 1 April 2026, for all businesses that voluntarily apply for GST registration.

IRAS would continue to consult industry partners on the initiative and carefully review the feedback received before announcing further details for the remaining GST-registered businesses. 

InvoiceNow solutions are finance and accounting software that allow the transmission of invoices between businesses via the InvoiceNow network. InvoiceNow solutions are connected to IRAS through Access Points (AP). This connection would enable the transmission of invoice data to IRAS via Application Programming Interface (API) technology. 

Invoices that are not issued using InvoiceNow (e.g. for sales made via point-of-sale systems) should also be recorded in InvoiceNow solutions and sent to IRAS.

The scope of invoice data covers the transactions with GST tax categories of: -

(1) Standard-rate supplies,

(2) Zero-rated supplies,

(3) Exempt supplies,

(4) Standard-rated purchases on which input tax claims are made or will be made,

(5) Zero-rated purchases

Excludes deemed supplies, goods exported without sales, reverse charge supplies and reverse charge purchases.

The invoice data must be transmitted to IRAS by the earlier of:

  • The date on which the relevant GST return is filed; or
  • The filing due date of the relevant GST return.

For more information on the GST InvoiceNow requirement, please check to the IRAS GST InvoiceNow Requirement webpage here.

SAP is an InvoiceNow solution for both Access Point Provider and InvoiceNow-Ready Solution Provider and is currently developing SAP Document and Reporting solution to support GST InvoiceNow requirement, please check the plan & update at regulatory change manager 

Once the solution is ready, the how-to-implement will be published here.

Get to know about Peppol eInvoices or InvoiceNow solution in Singapore, please check the blogs: -

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Singapore Peppol eInvoices (InvoiceNow) - Part 1

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Singapore Peppol eInvoices (InvoiceNow) - Part 2

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This blog is intended to provide Customers and Partners the guide or how-to implement SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition for Singapore GST InvoiceNow requirement.

Please share your feedback and thoughts in a comment. If you have questions about SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, you can post your question here.

entry.title: Be Part of Tomorrow: Join SAP's Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1, 2025!

In today’s fast-paced business world, staying ahead is more important than ever. The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative presents a unique opportunity to gain early insights into SAP’s product developments. By participating, you’ll work directly with our developers and product managers, helping shape the future of our products.

Register until March 28, 2025! Don’t miss out on the opportunity to have your voice heard and influence the next generation of SAP solutions.

entry.title: Unlocking Business Opportunities at Siemens through the Power of Localization and Clean Core
entry.content:
As a global business leader, navigating the complexities of local regulations and market specificities while maintaining a large-scale operation can be daunting. At SAP, we have seen the transformative power of localization — the strategic adaptation of business processes to meet local needs. Today, I want to share how Siemens is leveraging SAP's localization capabilities to streamline its global operations. We had the pleasure of speaking with two seasoned professionals at Siemens: Ms. Andrea Spandau and Mr. Uwe Stuerzenhofecker, experienced transformation leaders in the area of Tax and Customs processes. Their insights provide a deeper understanding of how the company is navigating this transformative process and the critical role of localization in ensuring global success.

Driving Enterprise Success - The Siemens Journey

Siemens serves various sectors, including industry, infrastructure, mobility, and healthcare. As a global powerhouse operating in more than 170 countries and boasting a rich history of innovation and reliability over its 175-year journey, Siemens has consistently adapted to the shifting business landscapes of digitalization and sustainability. The implementation of a clean core ERP system has been a game-changer. By aligning business processes with strategy and maintaining global standards, Siemens ensures lean operations and enhanced focus on delivering customer-centric solutions. This approach not only streamlines internal processes but significantly boosts customer satisfaction as clients experience tailored services that genuinely meet their local needs.

A key aspect of Siemens' transformation is the SHERPA X program, which aims to migrate the company's ERP systems to SAP S/4HANA. This ambitious project involves more than 1,000 legal entities across 60 different ERP systems, with SAP being the primary platform.

The Strategic Role of Harmonization and Localization

Localization is an integral part of the Siemens SAP S/4HANA transformation program. Siemens recognizes that a global template does not fit every country, and local legal requirements must be met. The localization process starts with deciding which country will be localized, followed by the creation of a country-specific blueprint that serves as a basis for future businesses operating in that country.

Siemens has a dedicated localization team that collaborates with regional experts to conduct fit-gap analyses and ensure that the template covers all legal requirements. This process involves a series of workshops and deep-dive sessions, engaging local experts to virtually discuss local legal requirements to be implemented for the end-to-end processes such as lead to cash, purchase to pay and record to report.

Siemens faces the challenge of aligning its vast operations across different regulatory, tax, and business landscapes. The solution? A robust localization strategy encompassed within their Siemens SAP S/4HANA transformation program. Here’s how they do it:

  1. Developing Country-Specific Blueprint: Starting with a baseline blueprint that encompasses all legal requirements of a country, Siemens crafts a foundational layer essential for compliance. This strategic move allows the company to confidently and swiftly enter new markets.
  2. Incorporating Business-Specific Adaptations: On top of this compliance layer, Siemens integrates specific business processes that are crucial for meeting the localized demands. This dual approach ensures that while the global standards are met, the solutions resonate well with local customers. Harmonization, in Siemens’ strategy, means standardizing core processes across the company while ensuring flexibility. The Siemens SAP S/4HANA transformation program focuses on creating a lean ERP core where indispensable functionalities reside, simplifying maintenance and integration of new technologies or processes.
  3. Ensuring Integrity and Consistency: A key aspect of Siemens’ strategy includes employing SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) to uphold data integrity across all their ERP systems. With MDG, Siemens streamlines data modifications which are only permissible in the central system, thereby maintaining consistency across all customer transactions and financial processes. This strategic governance assures that regardless of geographical boundaries, every operation within Siemens aligns with the core business standards.

A Beacon for Global Enterprises

One of the guiding principles of Siemens' localization approach is maintaining a lean ERP core. Siemens’ commitment to a clean core and comprehensive localization not only benefits their operation but also empowers their clients.

Siemens employs a range of SAP solutions, such as SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) for e-invoicing, SAP Global Trade Services (GTS) for customs and export control, and SAP Business Integrity Screening. These solutions work in harmony with the ERP system, providing a comprehensive and efficient platform for global operations.

The journey Siemens is on serves as an inspiring blueprint for many other global entities. By sharing their strategies and successes, we aim to embolden more enterprises to embrace and leverage localization in their business transformation endeavors. As we continue partnering with companies across various industries and regions, SAP remains dedicated to offering the essential tools and insights required for mastering the intricacies of global operations.

Summary

Siemens' journey towards digitalization and standardization is a testament to the company's commitment to staying ahead in an ever-evolving business landscape. By leveraging SAP's localization capabilities and maintaining a clean core, Siemens is well-positioned to navigate the complexities of global operations and deliver value to its customers.

As the Tax & Customs team continues to drive the specific localization requirements within the Siemens SAP S/4HANA transformation program, they are not only transforming Siemens' internal processes but also setting an example for other global enterprises looking to streamline their operations. The strategies and practices developed during this journey will undoubtedly serve as a valuable guide for other organizations aiming for global success. Through innovative localization and a focus on clean core principles, Siemens is paving the way for a more efficient, compliant, and customer-centric future.

entry.title: 統一發票在 SAP ERP 公有雲系統中的實施方案建議
entry.content:
在數位化時代，電子發票已成為企業提升流程效率和合規的重要工具。作為專注於 SAP ERP 公有雲的解決方案架構師，基於豐富的專案參與經驗，本文提供關於電子發票管理的深入洞見，旨在幫助企業用戶充分利用 SAP 產品實現統一發票的數位化管理。

 

電子發票流程的必要性

依據台灣財政部的法規，電子發票已被推薦作為標準格式。SAP ERP 公有雲產品提供強大的支持，成為企業在數位轉型中不可或缺的組成部分。在眾多實施專案中，企業面臨的挑戰通常在於如何有效整合電子發票到現有系統及流程中。為確保從紙質到電子系統的過渡順利，建議採用分階段實施策略。

 

理解並優化現有發票流程

在許多企業中，紙質發票的管理常常帶來操作上的繁瑣以及高錯誤率。當企業客製化的系統流程與功能中涉及紙質發票流程時，可能會遇到以下問題：

  • 整合難度：紙質和電子發票系統的整合可能涉及多重系統之間的信息同步和數據轉換，增加了技術復雜性。
  • 效率低下：處理紙質發票通常需要人工干預，如掃描和手動數據輸入，這可能導致效率降低。
  • 錯誤風險：手動過程中容易出現數據錯誤，可能造成財務報表的不準確。

建議企業選擇分開管理紙質和電子發票，並逐步推廣至全電子化進程。基於對 SAP ERP 公有雲的深入認識，系統中的嚴謹配置和測試至關重要，以免發生發票號碼等關鍵數據整合問題。

 

處理停用的電子計算機統一發票

自2021年以來，電子計算機統一發票已經停止適用，參考台灣當地財政部賦稅署公告。停止適用後，為了管理和查閱歷史交易數據，SAP 系統中仍可能出現與電子計算機發票相關的税碼設定。為了防止錯誤使用停用的税碼，專案顧問需要利用稅條件類型的設定，限制用戶只能選擇當前實際適用的銷項稅碼。將客戶稅分類碼進行調整，如用“E”來對應電子發票税碼，以取代傳統設計的“1”税碼，是一個有效的解決方案。

 

電子發票功能的全面展望

SAP ERP 公有雲提供一整套電子發票標準税碼，以協助企業滿足多樣化的稅務需求：

  • A5：零税率銷售，如出口商品。
  • A6：針對境內一般課稅。
  • A7：境內零稅。
  • A8：境內免稅/免開立電子發票銷售。
  • A9：用於境內特殊稅率。

在設計和實施這些功能時，建議企業激活 5XU 範圍項目，並參考以下的配置指南，以確保最大化利用這一數字化工具：

  • 客戶（銷售）電子發票相關配置指南
  • 電子發票類型與編碼配置指南
  • VAT 申報配置指南
  • 營業場所配置指南

 

預備未來的發票標準

根據電子發票資料交換標準訊息建置指引(MIG) ，從2026年起，電子發票需遵循 MIG4.1 標準。當前，SAP 的系統版本支援 MIG4.0 標準，產品研發團隊已經積極應對更新法規的要求，未來的系統更新已列入日程。建議企業提前規劃，以便順利適應法規變更。

免開立電子發票的場景處理

依照統一發票使用辦法第四條 ，某些免税商品可免開立統一發票。在這些情況下，SAP 標準流程並不會自動阻止電子發票的生成。因此，專案可通過增強 BADI EDOC_TW_IS_RELEVANT 實現客製化邏輯，以條件性地抑制電子發票的生成，從而達到業務需求。

 

多元税碼場景的應對方法

隨著 MIG4.0 標準的啟用，“F0401 平台存證開立發票”可在同一張憑證中包含應稅與免税或零税率的項目。SAP ERP 公有雲自 2502 版本起支持混合税率的電子發票。這意味著，在同一張憑證中可以包含應稅與免税或零税率的項目。企業可以利用此功能實現更多元的發票處理模式。詳細說明參見 Multiple Tax | SAP What's New Viewer。

但若企業管理的需求仍需對不同税率單獨開票，有兩種方案可預期實現：

  • 流程控制：規範操作流程，確保同一銷售訂單僅限使用同一税率，從根源上預防混合税率情況。
  • 技術增強：使用 BADI SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER 實施客製化邏輯，在銷售請款憑證生成時，按稅碼拆分請款憑證。需注意，根據 SAP 的設計，電子發票憑證需與銷售請款憑證保持一一對應關係。詳細參見 Forcing a Billing Document Split。

 

集成加值中心與 VAT 申報

在某些專案中，部分企業選擇透過加值中心進行 VAT 申報，這涉及如何將 SAP 系統的電子發票數據有效推送至加值中心。由於不同加值中心對電子資訊交互的要求略有差異，SAP 無法提供統一的解決方案，因此需客製化開發來匹配不同加值中心的技術需求，確保準確數據傳輸與符合規範。

專案實施可透過銷售流程憑證的相關 CDS 視圖讀取資料，並整合為電子發票申報集成所需的資料格式。以下博客詳細闡述了一則實施實例：嵌入式 SAC 客戶用例分享之加值中心集成（台灣地區本地化需求）。若您期望SAP能通过标准产品内容提供统整的台湾电子发票CDS视图，欢迎您通过贵司的 S-User 访问 Release Unified CDS Views for Taiwan e-Invoice Data Export and Integrations | SAP Customer Influence Portal ，投出您宝贵的一票。



結語

透過這些見解，企業在統一發票的數位化管理旅程中能夠獲得新的視角和實用策略。成功的電子發票應用促進業務流程改進，提升合規性，也是企業數位化中不可忽視的環節。欲進一步詳細了解 SAP 台灣電子發票標準方案，歡迎參考SAP Document Compliance 台灣電子發票解決方案。

entry.title: 台湾地区统一发票在 SAP ERP 公有云系统中的实施方案建议
entry.content:
在数字化时代，电子发票已成为企业提升流程效率和合规的重要工具。作为专注于 SAP ERP 公有云的解决方案架构师，基于丰富的项目参与经验，本文提供关于台湾地区统一发票管理的深入洞见，旨在帮助企业用户充分利用 SAP 产品实现统一发票的数字化管理。

 

电子发票流程的必要性

根据台湾财政部的法规，电子发票已被推荐作为统一发票标准格式。SAP ERP 公有云产品提供强大的支持，成为企业在数字化转型中不可或缺的组成部分。在众多实施项目中，企业面临的挑战通常在于如何有效整合电子发票到现有系统及流程中。为确保从纸质到电子系统的过渡顺利，建议采用分阶段实施策略。

 

理解并优化现有发票流程

在许多企业中，纸质发票的管理常常带来操作上的繁琐以及高错误率。当企业定制化的系统流程与功能中涉及纸质发票流程时，可能会遇到以下问题：

  • 整合难度：纸质和电子发票在系统层面的整合可能涉及多个系统之间的信息同步和数据转换，增加了技术复杂性。
  • 效率低下：处理纸质发票通常需要人工干预，如扫描和手动数据输入，这可能导致效率降低。
  • 错误风险：手动过程中容易出现数据错误，可能导致财务报表的不准确。

建议企业选择分开管理纸质和电子发票，并逐步推广至全电子化进程。基于对 SAP ERP 公有云的深入认识，系统中的严谨配置和测试至关重要，以防止发生发票号码等关键数据整合问题。

 

处理停用的电子计算机统一发票

自2021年以来，电子计算机统一发票已经停止适用，参考台湾当地财政部税务署公告。停止适用后，为了管理和查阅历史交易数据，SAP 系统中仍可能出现与电子计算机发票相关的税码设置。为了防止错误使用停用的税码，项目顾问需要利用税条件类型的设置，限制用户只能选择当前实际适用的销项税码。将客户税分类码进行调整，如用“E”来对应电子发票税码，以替代传统设计的“1”税码，是一个有效的解决方案。

 

电子发票功能的全面展望

SAP ERP 公有云提供一整套电子发票标准税码，以协助企业满足多样化的税务需求：

  • A5：零税率销售，如出口商品。
  • A6：针对境内一般课税。
  • A7：境内零税。
  • A8：境内免税/免开立电子发票销售。
  • A9：用于境内特殊税率。

在设计和实施这些功能时，建议企业激活 5XU 范围项目，并参考以下的配置指南，以确保最大化利用这一数字化工具：

  • 客户（销售）电子发票相关配置指南
  • 电子发票类型与编码配置指南
  • VAT 申报配置指南
  • 营业场所配置指南

 

预备未来的发票标准

根据电子发票数据交换标准信息构建指引(MIG)，从2026年起，电子发票需遵循 MIG4.1 标准。当前，SAP 的系统版本支持 MIG4.0 标准，产品研发团队已经积极应对更新法规的要求，未来的系统更新已列入日程。建议企业提前规划，以便顺利适应法规变更。

 

免开立电子发票的场景处理

依据统一发票使用办法第四条，某些免税商品可免开立统一发票。在这些情况下，SAP 标准流程并不会自动阻止电子发票的生成。因此，项目可通过增强 BADI EDOC_TW_IS_RELEVANT 实现定制化逻辑，以条件性地抑制电子发票的生成，从而满足业务需求。

 

多元税码场景的应对方法

随着 MIG4.0 标准的启动，“F0401 平台存证开立发票”可在同一张凭证中包含应税与免税或零税率的项目。SAP ERP 公有云自 2502 版本起支持混合税率的电子发票。这意味着，在同一张凭证中可以包含应税与免税或零税率的项目。企业可以利用此功能实现更多样的发票处理模式。详细说明参见 Multiple Tax | SAP What's New Viewer。

但若企业管理的需求仍需对不同税率单独开票，有两种方案可预期实现：

  • 流程控制：规范操作流程，确保同一销售订单仅限使用同一税率，从根源上预防混合税率情况。
  • 技术增强：使用 BADI SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER 实施定制化逻辑，在销售请款凭证生成时，按税码拆分请款凭证。需注意，根据 SAP 的设计，电子发票凭证需与销售请款凭证保持一一对应关系。详细参见 Forcing a Billing Document Split。

 

集成加值中心与 VAT 申报

在某些项目中，部分企业选择通过加值中心进行 VAT 申报，这涉及如何将 SAP 系统的电子发票数据有效推送至增值中心。由于不同加值中心对电子信息交互的要求略有差异，SAP 无法提供统一的解决方案符合所有加值中心的要求，因此需定制化开发以匹配不同加值中心的技术需求，确保准确数据传输与合规。

项目实施可通过销售流程凭证的相关 CDS 视图读取数据，并整合为电子发票申报集成所需的数据格式。以下博客详细阐述了一个实施实例：嵌入式 SAC 客户用例分享之加值中心集成（台湾地区本地化需求）。若您期望 SAP 能通过标准产品内容提供统合的台湾电子发票 CDS 视图，欢迎您通过贵司的 S-User 访问 Release Unified CDS Views for Taiwan e-Invoice Data Export and Integrations | SAP Customer Influence Portal，投出您宝贵的一票。



结语

通过这些洞见，企业在统一发票的数字化管理旅程中能够获得新的视角和实用策略。成功的电子发票应用促进业务流程改进，提升合规性，也是企业数字化中不可忽视的环节。欲进一步详细了解 SAP 台湾电子发票标准方案，欢迎参考SAP Document Compliance 台湾电子发票解决方案。

entry.title: Expert Insights on Adopting Solutions for Taiwan's Government Uniform Invoices (GUI)
entry.content:
In the digital era, electronic invoicing has become an important tool for enterprises to improve process efficiency and compliance. As a solution architect focusing on SAP ERP public cloud, based on rich project participation experience, by this blog I'd like to provide in-depth insights into Taiwan Government Uniform Invoice (GUI) management, aiming to help enterprise users fully utilize SAP products to achieve unified invoice digital management.

 

Necessity of Electronic Invoice Processes

According to Taiwan Ministry of Finance regulations, electronic invoices have been recommended as a standard format. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provide strong support, becoming an indispensable component in the digital transformation of enterprises. In numerous implementation projects, the challenge for enterprises usually lies in effectively integrating electronic invoices into existing systems and processes. To ensure a smooth transition from paper to electronic systems, a phased implementation strategy is recommended.

 

Understanding and Optimizing Existing Invoice Processes

In many enterprises, the management of paper invoices often leads to operational complexity and high error rates. When custom system processes and functions involve paper invoice processes, the following issues may occur:

  • Integration difficulty: Integration of paper and electronic invoice systems may involve information synchronization and data conversion between multiple systems, increasing technical complexity.
  • Low efficiency: Processing paper invoices often requires manual intervention, such as scanning and manual data input, which may reduce efficiency.
  • Error risk: Manual processes are prone to data errors, which may lead to inaccuracies in financial statements.

It is recommended that enterprises manage paper and electronic invoices separately and gradually transition to a fully electronic process. Based on an in-depth understanding of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, meticulous system configuration and testing are crucial to prevent key data integration issues such as invoice numbers.

 

Handling of Disabled Computerized Uniform Invoices

Since 2021, computerized uniform invoices have been discontinued. Refer to Taiwan local Ministry of Finance Taxation Bureau announcement. After discontinuation, for managing and reviewing historical transaction data, SAP systems may still include tax code settings related to computerized invoices. To prevent incorrect use of discontinued tax codes, project consultants need to use tax condition type settings, restricting users to select only currently applicable output tax codes. Adjusting customer tax classification codes, such as using "E" to correspond to electronic invoice tax codes to replace the traditionally designed "1" tax code, is an effective solution.

 

Comprehensive Outlook on Electronic Invoice Functions

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides a complete set of standard electronic invoice tax codes to help enterprises meet diverse tax needs:

  • A5: Zero tax rate sales, such as export goods.
  • A6: General taxation within Taiwan region.
  • A7: Zero tax within Taiwan region.
  • A8: Tax-exempt/sales w/o issuing electronic invoices withinTaiwan region.
  • A9: Used for special tax rates withinTaiwan region.

In designing and implementing these functions, it is recommended that enterprises activate the 5XU scope item and refer to the following configuration guides to maximize the use of this digital tool:

  • Customer (Sales) Electronic Invoice Configuration Guide
  • Electronic Invoice Type and Coding Configuration Guide
  • VAT Reporting Configuration Guide
  • Business Place Configuration Guide

 

Preparing for Future Invoice Standards

According to Electronic Invoice Data Exchange Standard Message Implementation Guide (MIG), from 2026 onwards, electronic invoices must comply with the MIG4.1 standard. The current SAP system version supports MIG4.0 standard, and the product development team is actively responding to the requirements of updated regulations, with future system updates already scheduled. Enterprises are advised to plan ahead to smoothly adapt to regulatory changes.

 

Handling Scenarios Exempt from Issuing Electronic Invoices

According to the 4th item of Taiwan Government Uniform Invoice Usage Regulations, some tax-exempt goods can be exempt from issuing GUI. In these cases, the SAP standard process does not automatically prevent the generation of electronic invoices. Thus, projects can achieve custom logic through enhancing BADI EDOC_TW_IS_RELEVANT to conditionally suppress the generation of electronic invoices, thereby meeting business needs.

 

Handling Mix Tax Codes Scenarios

With the activation of the MIG4.0 standard, "F0401 platform certificate invoice" can contain taxable and tax-exempt or zero-rated items in the same certificate. With the release of 2502, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition supports mixed tax rate electronic invoices. This means that taxable and tax-exempt or zero-rated items can be included in the same invoice. Enterprises can utilize this functionality to achieve more diverse invoice processing modes. For more detailed instructions, refer to Multiple Tax | SAP What's New Viewer.

However, if the enterprise's management needs still require separate invoicing for different tax rates, there are two workaround solutions:

  • Process control: Standardize the business operation process to ensure that the same sales order is limited to using the same tax rate, preventing the situation of mixed tax rates from the source.
  • Technical enhancement: Apply customized logic using BADI SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER to split billing documents by tax code when generating sales billing documents. Note that, according to SAP's design, electronic invoice documents need to maintain a one-to-one correspondence with sales billing documents. For more details, refer to Forcing a Billing Document Split.

 

Integrating Value-added Center with VAT Declaration

In certain projects, some enterprises choose to conduct VAT declaration through value-added centers, which involves how to effectively push electronic invoice data from the SAP system to the value-added center. Since different value-added centers have slightly differing requirements for electronic information interaction, SAP cannot provide a unified solution. Therefore, custom development is required to match the technical needs of different value-added centers, ensuring accurate data transmission and compliance.

Project implementation can read data through the relevant CDS views of the sales/billing documents and integrate them into the data format required for electronic invoice declaration integration. The following blog details an implementation example: Embedded SAC Customer Case Sharing on Value-added Center Integration (Taiwan Region Localization Needs). If you expect SAP to provide unified Taiwan electronic invoice CDS views through standard product content, we welcome you to visit Release Unified CDS Views for Taiwan e-Invoice Data Export and Integrations | SAP Customer Influence Portal with your company's S-User and cast your valuable vote.



Conclusion

Through these insights, enterprises can gain new perspectives and practical strategies in the digital management journey of unified invoices. Successful electronic invoice applications promote business process improvements and enhance compliance, which are crucial aspects of enterprise digitalization. To further understand SAP's Taiwan electronic invoice standard solutions, you are welcome to refer to SAP Document Compliance Taiwan e-Invoice Solution.

entry.title: Are You Ready for the Mandatory Shift to E-invoicing in Belgium?
entry.content:
The world of invoicing is evolving, and e-invoicing is no longer just a trend, it is a legal requirement that businesses must follow.  Also, in Belgium the commitment to implement e-invoicing broadly has become real.  The law for mandatory domestic B2B e-invoicing in Belgium was published in the Belgian Official Journal on February 20, 2024 and will be effective on January 1, 2026.

The shift to mandatory e-invoicing is not just about business efficiency anymore, it is about adhering to stringent tax regulations and ensuring that your invoicing processes are compliant with legislative requirements.  This significant change presents an urgent need for businesses to ensure their processes are well-prepared for the upcoming changes and to define a strong compliance strategy with the right tools.

Why It’s Time to Act Now: The Transition to E-invoicing

The shift from traditional invoicing methods to e-invoicing is not simply a matter of adopting a more efficient tool, it is a matter of staying compliant with tax requirements.  It is essential for companies to realize that digitalization of invoices is no longer optional, and getting ahead of this change will save both time and resources in the long run.  While some companies may have used EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) to enhance business efficiency in the past, e-invoicing is now the legal expectation.

The shift to e-invoicing mandates transcends borders.  A lot of countries already adopted e-invoicing legislation.  Other countries are now taking significant steps forward to the implementation of e-invoicing mandates.  In Belgium, for example, the B2B e-invoicing mandate will enter into force on January 1, 2026.  Many other countries will follow this trend, such as France, Germany, etc.  This means that companies need a solution that can scale to meet global compliance requirements.

How SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Helps You Stay Compliant Globally

The core objective is to make sure businesses are aligned with the requirements set out by governments for electronic invoicing.

This is where SAP Document and Reporting Compliance comes into play.  SAP Document and Reporting Compliance is designed to help businesses navigate the complexities of e-invoicing and ensure seamless compliance across multiple jurisdictions.  With this solution companies can easily send and receive e-invoices, managing this entire process end-to-end with minimal effort.  The system integrates directly into your existing SAP landscape, making the transition as smooth as possible.

With SAP Document and Reporting Compliance there is a global approach for coping with e-invoicing mandates.  Our solution already supports over 55 countries, and many more countries are on the development roadmap, such as Belgium, France, Germany, etc.  This means whether you’re operating in the EU, in Latin America, or beyond, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance  help you to meeting local e-invoicing requirements, no matter where you do business.

Next to the support of e-invoicing mandates, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance also manages regulatory reporting requirements from many countries worldwide.  For Belgium, for instance, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance supports the filing of the VAT returns (periodic VAT return, intra-Community statement and annual sales list), the balance of payments reporting, etc.

By providing this extensive global coverage, SAP ensures your company is always up to date with the latest regulatory requirements.  The solution is built with flexibility in mind, allowing it to handle various invoicing scenarios across different regions.

In addition, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance provides robust features for data validation, error handling, and reporting, further increasing automation and efficiency in your invoicing processes.  The system’s end-to-end capabilities make it the ideal solution for businesses looking to minimize manual work while ensuring they meet complex legal requirements.

Stay Up to Date with Regulatory Change Manager App of SAP

Regulatory requirements are continuously evolving, and it is essential for businesses to stay on top of these changes.  Through the Regulatory Change Manager app (regulatory change manager) you can stay updated with SAP’s analysis of how these changes impact their solutions.

Conclusion: Don’t Wait — Get Ready for the Future of E-invoicing Today

As increasing digitalization in tax administration is reshaping conventional practices and e-invoicing regulations are constantly evolving, now is the time to act.  It is time to equip your company with the right tools and ensure you are fully prepared for the mandatory shift to e-invoicing.  Let SAP Document and Reporting Compliance guide you every step of the way, so you can focus on growing your business, not worrying about compliance.

 

Stay tuned for more in the coming weeks and share any questions you may have in the comments section below!

 

If you want to learn about the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance in your country and engage with SAP experts, join your regional collaboration group.

Check out the SAP Community for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

 

You can find more details on the e-invocing in our dedicated blogs:

  • Revolutionizing Taxation: Navigating VAT in the Digital Age (ViDA)
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Cloud or On-Premise? Not an “either or” option, but a streamlined solution for electronic compliance!
  • I received an electronic supplier invoice, what now?

entry.title: Get Involved: Help Shape the Future of SAP Globalization in 2025
entry.content:
As businesses expand globally, navigating local regulations, legal mandates, and statutory reporting becomes complex. SAP Globalization is here to help by providing a comprehensive localization solution across SAP’s portfolio, ensuring companies can succeed both locally and globally.

The first cycle of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative, offers you as SAP customers and partners the chance to directly influence future localization features. By joining, you’ll exchange feedback with SAP product management to shape the future of SAP Globalization.

Registration for SAP Globalization projects is open until March 28, 2025. Don’t miss out on this opportunity to help define the future of global business solutions. Follow the links below to discover the exciting opportunities or check out the full list here:

  • Australia and New Zealand: GST requirement for Down payments
  • Australia: Modified Payment Times Report - AASB 8 and Taxable Payments Annual Report
  • ISO 21378 Audit Data Collection Standard
  • Planned enhancements to Run statutory reports to handle Draft Report reconciliation
  • Sales and Use Tax Reporting in United States - User Research
  • Sales and Use Tax payments in United States

We believe that collaboration is key to sparking innovation. By working alongside our customers and partners, we can jointly shape the future of SAP’s products and transform business operations.

Let’s take this journey together. Together, we have the power to drive progress and transform industries.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2025!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: SAP ERP公有云台湾本地化-代扣税实施建议
entry.content:
引言：由于SAP ERP公有云标准并没有提供台湾本地化的代扣税功能，本篇博客主要是基于对其他国家的参考来实现的，欢迎各位读者能够提出更多的意见和建议。

目录：

  • 代扣税介绍
  • 过账代扣税的业务场景
  • 系统中配置代扣税
  • 代扣税功能演示
  • 代扣税报表
  • 结论
  • 参考信息

___________________________________________________________________________

  • 代扣税介绍

代扣税扣减由适用国家/地区的法定要求决定，因此纯粹是一个本地化主题。在SAP ERP公有云中，通过范围项目 1J2-高级合规性报表进行处理。

代扣税是在某些国家/地区开始付款流时收取的税。通常，应纳税方不会直接向税务部门缴纳代扣税。当有权扣减代扣税的客户支付应缴纳代扣税的供应商发票时，客户会从付款金额中减去代扣税金额。然后，客户直接向相应的税务部门缴纳代扣税。但对于自代扣例外，在这种情况下，受税款约束的供应商有权直接向相关部门支付税款。

在台湾地区，代扣税是公司代扣的居民或非居民收入付款（付款方）的金额。如果您要支付非本地（国外）供应商，则需要预扣发票金额的一定百分比并向税务局支付税表，以及要支付给国外供应商的余额。

涵盖如下付款：

  • 股息
  • 利息
  • 租赁收入
  • 版税
  • 技术费用、服务付款
  • 工资和薪水
  • 佣金

台湾的代扣税制度适用于居民和非居民所赚取的各种收入，按收入类型和纳税人的居住身份确定税率和规定。每种类型的付款都适用不同的代扣税率，如果台湾与非居民（外国方）是税收居民的国家/地区之间存在双重税收协议，则优惠税率可能适用。下表详细说明了台湾代扣税系统中不同付款的相关表单。

收入类型

居民个人

非居民个人

外国公司

股息

20%

21%

21%

利息

10%

20%

20%

租赁收入

10%

20%

20%

版税

10%

20%

20%

专业服务费

10%

20%(有例外)

20%

薪水和工资

6%-18%

18% (如果<183 天停留)

不适用

佣金

-

20%

20%

参考文末链接【3】

代扣税举例：台湾公司向外国公司采购专业服务费税前金额为 5000 美元，外国公司将承担预扣税，因此，台湾公司只需要向国外公司支付4000美 元，扣除的1000美 元（5000 美元的 20%）应该支付给税务部门。

  • 过账代扣税的业务场景
  1. 根据每个国家/地区的法律要求，在不同阶段计算代扣税并将其过账到相应的代扣税科目。通常，代扣税是在过账付款的同时过账的。换言之，付款（应付账款）或收款（应收账款）将减去代扣税金额。
  2. 在应付账款中，供应商应纳税，公司有义务扣减代扣税并代表供应商将其支付给税务部门。在应收账款中，公司本身需要缴税。与此公司有业务往来的客户扣减代扣税，并代表公司将其支付给税务部门。在这两种情况下，应纳税人/实体的业务伙伴都会扣减税款并将其支付给税务部门。
  3. 在某些国家/地区（例如巴西、菲律宾和西班牙），代扣税可以或必须在过账发票时过账。这意味着应收账款或应付账款金额会减去代扣税金额。
  4. 在某些国家/地区（例如阿根廷和意大利），某些业务交易需要其他类型的过账。代扣税将作为抵销分录过账到费用科目，而不是按代扣税金额减少的付款金额。维护代扣税代码时为此进行相关设置。
  5. 重置已清算项目时，如果清算凭证包含代扣税数据，则会自动冲销。对于含累计的代扣税类型，如果冲销付款凭证，则会调整累计基础金额。
  6. 本博客中，会示例应付发票时点过账代扣税及付款时点过账代扣税这两种场景。

 

  • 系统中配置代扣税

     1.  激活代扣税

要在SAP ERP公有云中使用代扣税（也称为扩展代扣税），必须先将其激活。

2. 定义代扣税代码– 106576

代扣税代码用于标识不同的代扣税类型。

3. 为付款过账定义代扣税类型– 104226

预扣税可以在两个时间点扣除；可以在开票时或付款时扣除。因此，对于此代扣税类型，将创建一个代扣税类型用于付款，另一个用于发票。

4. 为发票过账定义代扣税类型– 104225

预扣税可以在两个时间点扣除；可以在开票时或付款时扣除。因此，对于此代扣税类型，将创建一个代扣税类型用于付款，另一个用于发票。

5. 为公司代码分配代扣税类型– 103914

6. 定义代扣税码- 104227

在代扣税代码中，维护不同的税率并维护对应扣减的基础。

7. 定义代扣税码生效最小/最大限定金额 – 103915

如果需要，可以定义代扣税码生效最小/最大限定金额，如果不指定任何金额，则代表代扣税没有上下限金额的限定。

8. 将代扣税代码分配给代扣税码 – 105553

9. 为代扣税类型分配汇率类型 - 104400

10. 为过账代扣税分配总账科目 – 100297

11. 检查代扣税配置

 

  • 代扣税功能演示

SAP ERP公有云中代扣税也称为扩展代扣税，包含代扣税类型和代扣税代码。代扣税类型表示基本计算规则。代扣税代码代表这些规则的特定功能，尤其是百分率。

如果特定交易需要多种代扣税，则在SAP ERP公有云系统中通过定义多个代扣税类型来涵盖这一点。输入行项目时，可以为每种代扣税类型输入代扣税数据。但请注意，如果发票中有两组或多组代扣税代码和类型，则必须决定所有建议的代扣税代码和类型是否与交易相关。如果不计算和过账某些建议的代扣税，则必须移除与交易无关的代扣税代码。

 

1. 在发票时点过账代扣税

1). 维护业务伙伴主数据

从如上截屏中能够看到供应商代扣税类型设定为发票过账时点生成代扣税行项目，代扣税码为T1即20%的代扣税率。

2). 过账供应商发票

查看模拟生成的财务分录：

查看到生成了代扣税凭证分录，以抵减应付账款金额。

2. 付款时点过账代扣税

1). 维护业务伙伴主数据

从如上截屏中能够看到供应商代扣税类型设定为付款时点生成代扣税行项目，代扣税码为T1即20%的代扣税率。

2). 过账供应商发票

查看模拟生成的财务分录：

3). 对供应商发票付款

模拟付款凭证，可以看到付款时点生成了下图的代扣税行项目。

目前，对于台湾的代扣税过账时点，客户可以根据需求参考应用对应的场景。

 

  • 代扣税报表

对于某些国家/地区，代扣税报表根据从供应商凭证和客户凭证中收集的信息生成，例如已清算发票、供应商贷记凭证、预付定金和已清算贷记凭证。系统生成可提交给政府部门的文本文件。某些国家/地区的凭证和报表合规性(DRC)功能被激活后，可以使用运行法定报表生成代扣税报表。

例如，如下是美国的代扣税报表：

Withholding Tax (1099-MISC) Report for the US

如下是比利时的代扣税报表信息：

Withholding Tax Report in Belgium

但是目前SAP ERP公有云还没有为台湾提供标准的代扣税申报报表，可以使用如下CDS View来获取相应的代扣税信息：

  • I_SupplierWithHoldingTax ：供应商代扣税
  • I_CustomerWithTax ：客户代扣税
  • I_WithholdingTaxCode ：代扣税码
  • I_SuplrInvcHeaderWhldgTaxAPI01 (供应商发票的代扣税)

现在已经有台湾客户发送代扣税申报报表的需求给SAP ERP公有云产品部门，期待未来台湾本地化功能中代扣税申报报表的呈现。客户请求详细请参考如下链接：

Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence

在CE2502版本开始SAP ERP公有云提供了一个新的应用程序“代扣税分析”，该应用程序能够回答一个问题，那就是“如何计算此交易的代扣税？”，如下是德国公司代码的范例：

对于该应用的信息，请参考帮助文档：Withholding Tax Calculation Analysis | SAP Help Portal

然而，该应用暂时不支持台湾地区使用，会提示如下信息：

期待未来该应用可以开放给台湾使用。

 

  • 结论

在台湾地区，代扣税适用于不同收入类型，例如台湾地区内部生成的股息、租金、利息和版税。其重要性在于确保及时征税、简化税务管理、执行遵约。

在每月的第十天之前，代扣代缴税申报方应向财政部缴纳上个月扣缴的税款，并于下一纳税年度的 1 月之前向税务局提交扣缴证明，具体说明上一年缴纳税款和扣缴的税款。如果在 1 月份有连续的公共节假日，则提交扣缴证明的时期应延长至 2 月 5 日。对于向非居民或在 ROC 中没有常设机构的外国企业支付的收入，代扣代缴税代理应在预扣税款后向财政部支付扣缴的税款，并在扣缴后十天内向税务局提交代扣代缴税报表和收据。参考文末链接【4】

与台湾许多税种一样，对税务申报的误解或错过截止期限，都会给在台内外经营的外国公司造成经济损失。

 

  • 参考信息
  1. Help Portal Documentation | Withholding Tax
  2.  Virtual Data Model and CDS Views | Withholding Tax for Supplier Invoice
  3. Taiwan withholding Tax Guide | Tax | SongjerCPA
  4. When shall a withholding agent file returns and make payments of tax withheld?
  5. Withholding Tax (1099-MISC) Report in the US
  6. Withholding Tax Report in Belgium

entry.title: SAP ERP公有雲台灣本土化 - 扣繳稅方案建議
entry.content:
簡介：由於SAP ERP公有雲標準並未提供針對台灣本土化的扣繳稅功能，因此此部落格主要根據來自其他國家的參考內容，歡迎讀者提出其他意見及建議。

目錄：

  • 扣繳稅簡介
  • 過帳扣繳稅的方案
  • 組態系統中的扣繳稅
  • 扣繳稅功能展示
  • 扣繳稅報表
  • 結論
  • 參考資訊

_______________________________________________________________

  • 扣繳稅簡介

由於扣繳稅扣除是根據適用國家的法定需求所規定，因此這純粹是本土化主題。在SAP ERP公有雲中，這會透過範圍項目 1J2- advanced Compliance Reporting 處理。

扣繳稅為部份國家/地區付款流程開始時收取的稅。一般而言，課稅方不會直接向稅捐機構支付扣繳稅。當授權扣除扣繳稅的客戶支付應付扣繳稅的供應商發票時，客戶會從付款金額中扣除扣繳稅額。接著，客戶會直接將扣繳稅支付給個別稅捐機構。然而，針對自行扣繳，視情況而定，供應商有權直接向機關支付稅款。

在台灣，扣繳稅為貴公司扣繳的居民或非居民所得稅 (付款人) 金額。若您正在支付非本地 (國外) 供應商，則必須扣繳發票金額的特定百分比並支付稅務表單給稅捐機關，以及要支付給國外供應商的餘額。

涵蓋下列付款：

  • 股利
  • 利息
  • 租賃收入
  • 版稅
  • 技術費用、服務付款
  • 工資和薪資
  • 佣金

台灣的代扣稅制，適用於居民與非居民所得的各種收入，按納稅人的收入類型和居住狀態確定稅率和規定。不同扣繳稅率適用於每種支付，如果台灣與非居民（外國人）為稅務居民的國家簽訂雙重徵稅協議，則適用優惠稅率。下列表格詳細說明台灣扣繳稅系統中不同付款的相關表單。

收入類型

居民個人

非居民個人

外國公司

股利

20%

21%

21%

利息

10%

20%

20%

租賃收入

10%

20%

20%

版稅

10%

20%

20%

專業費用

10%

20%〈有異常〉

20%

薪資和工資

6%-18%

18% (若 <183 天住宿)

不適用

佣金

-

20%

20%

參考連結 [3]

扣繳稅款範例：台灣企業向稅前5000元的外商購買專業服務，外商將承擔扣繳稅，因此台灣企業只需向外國企業支付4000美元，應向稅務機關繳納1000美元（折合5,000美元20%）的扣除。

  • 過帳扣繳稅的方案
  1. 根據各國家/地區的法律規定，扣繳稅會在不同階段進行計算，並過帳至對應的扣繳稅科目。作為規則，扣繳稅會在過帳付款的同時過帳。換句話說，扣繳稅額會減少付款 (應付帳款) 或收款 (應收帳款)。
  2. 在應付帳款中，供應商可稅，且公司有義務代表供應商扣除扣繳稅並支付給稅捐機構。在應收帳款中，公司本身需要課稅。與此公司進行業務的客戶可扣除扣繳稅，並代表公司支付給稅捐機構。在這兩種情況下，應稅人/實體的業務夥伴皆會扣除稅並支付給稅捐機構。
  3. 在某些國家/地區 (例如巴西、菲律賓和西班牙)，扣繳稅可在過帳發票時或必須過帳。這表示扣繳稅額會減少應收帳款或應付帳款金額。
  4. 在部份國家/地區 (例如阿根廷和義大利)，部份商業交易需要其他類型的過帳。扣繳稅會過帳為費用科目的沖銷分錄，而非扣繳稅金額減少的付款金額。維護扣繳稅碼時，您可進行相關設定。
  5. 重設結清的項目時，若結清文件包含扣繳稅資料，則會自動迴轉。針對含累計的扣繳稅類型，若已迴轉付款文件，則會調整累計基礎金額。
  6. 在此部落格中，範例 A/P 發票中會顯示付款時過帳扣繳稅和過帳扣繳稅的兩個方案。
  • 組態系統中的扣繳稅

1. 啟用扣繳稅 - 104228

若要在SAP ERP公有雲中使用扣繳稅 (也稱為延伸扣繳稅)，您必須先啟用。

2. 定義扣繳稅碼 – 106576

扣繳稅碼用於識別不同的扣繳稅類型。

3. 定義付款過帳的扣繳稅類型 – 104226

扣繳稅可於兩點扣除；可於開立發票時或付款時扣除。因此，針對此扣繳稅類型，系統會針對付款建立一個扣繳稅類型，而另一個用於發票。

4. 定義發票過帳的扣繳稅類型 – 104225

扣繳稅可於兩點扣除；可於開立發票時或付款時扣除。因此，針對此扣繳稅類型，系統會針對付款建立一個扣繳稅類型，而另一個用於發票。

5. 將扣繳稅類型指派給公司代碼 – 103914

6. 定義扣繳稅碼 - 104227

在扣繳稅碼中維護不同稅率，並維護對應扣除的基準。

7. 定義扣繳稅碼有效最小/最大限制金額 – 103915

您可視需要定義扣繳稅碼有效的最小/最大限制金額，或者若未指定金額，則扣繳稅額沒有下限和下限的金額限制。

8. 將扣繳稅碼指派給扣繳稅代碼 – 105553

9. 將匯率類型指派給扣繳稅類型 - 104400

10. 指派過帳扣繳稅的總帳科目 – 100297

11. 檢查扣繳稅組態

①檢查公司代碼

②檢查業務夥伴

③檢查扣繳稅類型

④模擬計算

 

  • 扣繳稅功能展示

SAP ERP公有雲中的扣繳稅也稱為延伸扣繳稅，並包含扣繳稅類型和扣繳稅碼。扣繳稅類型代表基礎計算規則。扣繳稅碼代表這些規則的特定功能，特別是百分比。

若特定交易需要一個以上的扣繳稅，則 SAP 系統中會透過定義數個扣繳稅類型來涵蓋此扣繳稅。輸入細目時，您可以輸入每個扣繳稅類型的扣繳稅資料。請注意，若發票中有兩個或多個扣繳稅代碼和類型集，則必須決定所有建議的扣繳稅碼和類型是否與交易相關。若未計算並過帳部份建議的扣繳稅，則必須移除與交易無關的扣繳稅碼。

1. 於發票點過帳扣繳稅方案

    1) 維護業務夥伴主檔資料

在上述擷取畫面中，您可看到供應商扣繳稅類型已設為發票過帳的時間點，以產生扣繳稅代碼 T1 (扣繳稅率為 20%) 的扣繳稅明細項目。

2). 過帳供應商發票

檢視模擬產生的財務輸入項：

您會看到扣繳稅文件輸入項已產生，以減少應付金額。

2. 於付款點過帳扣繳稅

    1). 維護業務夥伴主檔資料

在上述擷取畫面中，您可看到已將供應商扣繳稅類型設定為付款點，以產生扣繳稅代碼 T1 的扣繳稅明細項目 (扣繳稅率為 20%)。

2). 過帳供應商發票

檢視模擬產生的財務輸入項：

3). 供應商發票付款

模擬付款文件，您可在下列數字中查看付款點產生扣繳稅明細項目。

針對台灣的扣繳稅過帳點，客戶目前可根據需求參考對應方案。

 

  • 扣繳稅報表

針對部份國家/地區，扣繳稅報表會根據自供應商和客戶文件收集的資訊產生，例如結清發票、供應商貸項通知單、訂金和結清的貸項通知單。系統會產生可提交給政府機關的內文檔案。部份國家/地區的 Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) 功能啟用後，您可使用 [執行法定報表] 產生扣繳稅報表。

例如，美國的扣繳稅報表如下：

美國扣繳稅 (1099-MISC) 報表

比利時的扣繳稅申報資訊如下：

比利時扣繳稅報表

然而，SAP 尚未提供台灣的標準扣繳稅申報報表，且可使用下列 CDS 檢視以取得對應扣繳稅資訊：

  • I_SupplierWithHoldingTax：供應商扣繳稅
  • I_CustomerWithTax：客戶扣繳稅
  • I_WithholdingTaxCode：扣繳稅碼
  • I_SuplrInvcHeaderWhldgTaxAPI01: 供應商發票上的扣繳稅

現在台灣客戶需要將扣繳稅申報報表傳送給SAP ERP公有雲產品部門，並期待未來台灣本土化功能中的扣繳稅申報報表呈現。如需客戶請求，請參閱下列詳細連結：

改善請求明細 - 客戶影響

從 CE2502 開始，SAP ERP公有雲提供新的應用程式「扣繳稅分析」，可回答問題「要如何計算此交易的扣繳稅？」下列是德國公司代碼的範例：

如需有關應用程式的資訊，請參閱輔助說明文件： 扣繳稅計算分析 | SAP Help Portal

然而，此應用程式暫時不支援台灣使用，提示下列資訊：

希望未來將開放該應用程式在台灣使用。

 

  • 結論

在台灣地區，扣繳稅適用於不同的收入類型，例如台灣內部產生的股利、租金、利息和權利金。要確保及時納稅，簡化稅收管理，落實各項合規制度。

在每月第十天前，扣繳稅申報人應當向財政部繳納稅款上個月，並在下一納稅年度1月前向稅務機關提交扣繳證明，具體規定上一年度繳納稅款和代扣稅款。如果1月連續有國定假日，則提交扣繳憑證的期間應延長至2月5日。對於向非居民或者外國企業繳納的在中華民國境內沒有常設機構的收入，扣繳代理人應當在代扣稅款後向財政部繳納代扣稅款，並在代扣後十日內向稅務機關提交扣繳稅款申報和收據。參考連結 [4]

像台灣很多稅種一樣，對報稅的誤解和錯過期限，可能會給台灣境內外經營的外商造成經濟損失。

 

  • 參考資訊
  • Help Portal 紀錄文件 | 扣繳稅
  •  虛擬資料模型和 CDS 檢視 | 供應商發票扣繳稅
  • 台灣扣繳稅指南 | 稅 | SongjerCPA
  • 持有代理人何時應退回並支付扣繳稅？
  • 美國扣繳稅 (1099-MISC) 報表
  • 比利時扣繳稅報表

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Taiwan Localization - Workaround Solution for Withholding Tax
entry.content:
Introduction: Since the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition standard does not provide withholding tax functionality localized for Taiwan, this blog is based mainly on references from other countries, and readers are welcome to make additional comments and suggestions.

Catalog:

  • Withholding Tax Introduction
  • Scenario for Posting Withholding Tax
  • Configure Withholding Tax in the System
  • Withholding Tax Demos
  • Report for Withholding Tax
  • Conclusion
  • Reference Information

______________________________________________________________

 

  • Withholding Tax Introduction

Withholding Tax deduction is dictated by the statutory requirements of the applicable country and hence it is purely a localization topic. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition it is addressed through the scope item 1J2- Advance Compliance Reporting.

Withholding tax is the tax that is charged when the payment flow starts in some countries/regions. Generally, the taxable party does not pay withholding tax directly to the tax authority. When a customer authorized to deduct withholding tax pays a vendor invoice that is subject to withholding tax, the customer subtracts the withholding tax amount from the payment amount. The customer then pays the withholding tax directly to the tax authority. However, for self-withholding, suppliers subject to taxes have the right to pay taxes directly to the authorities in this case.

In Taiwan, withholding tax is the amount of resident or non-resident income payments (payers) withheld by your company. If you are paying a non-local (foreign) supplier, you need to withhold a certain percentage of the invoice amount and pay the tax form to the tax office, as well as the balance to be paid to the foreign supplier.

The following payments are covered:

  • Dividends
  • Interest
  • Rental Income
  • Royalties
  • Payments for technical fees, services
  • Wages and Salaries
  • Commissions

Taiwan's withholding tax system applies to the various income earned by residents and non-residents, determining tax rates and regulations by income type and residence status of taxpayers. Different withholding tax rates apply for each type of payment and if there is a double tax agreement between Taiwan and a country in which a non-resident (foreign party) is a tax resident, the reduced tax rate may apply. The following table details the relevant forms for the different payments in the Taiwan withholding tax system.

Reference link [3]

Withholding tax example: Taiwan companies purchase professional services from foreign companies with an amount of $5,000 before tax, foreign companies will bear withholding tax, so Taiwan companies only need to pay US$4,000 to foreign companies, and the deduction of 1,000 US dollars (20% of $5,000 USD) should be paid to the tax authorities.

  • Scenario for Posting Withholding Tax
  1. Depending on the legal requirements of each country/region, withholding tax is calculated at different stages and posted to the corresponding withholding tax accounts. As a rule, withholding tax is posted at the same time as the payment is posted. In other words, the payment (payable) or incoming payment (receivable) is reduced by the withholding tax amount.
  2. In accounts payable, the supplier is excisable and the company is obliged to deduct withholding tax and pay it to the tax authorities on behalf of the supplier. In accounts receivable, the company itself is subject to tax. Customers who do business with this company deduct withholding tax and pay them to the tax authorities on behalf of the company. In both cases, the business partner of the taxable person/entity deducts the tax and pays it to the tax authority.
  3. In some countries/regions, such as Brazil, the Philippines, and Spain, withholding tax can or must be posted when the invoice is posted. This means that the receivable or payable amount is reduced by the withholding tax amount.
  4. In some countries/regions, such as Argentina and Italy, some business transactions require other types of postings. Withholding tax is posted as an offsetting entry to the expense account instead of the payment amount reduced by the withholding tax amount. You make the relevant settings for this when maintaining withholding tax codes.
  5. When resetting cleared items, the clearing document is automatically reversed if it contains withholding tax data. For withholding tax types with accumulation, the accumulated base amount is adjusted if the payment document is reversed.
  6. In this blog, the two scenarios of posting withholding tax at point of payment and posting withholding tax at point of payment are shown in the examples for Account Payable.

 

  • Configure Withholding Tax in the System

1. Activate Withholding Tax - 104228

To use withholding tax in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition (also known as extended withholding tax), you must first activate it.

2. Define Withholding Tax Keys – 106576

Withholding tax codes are used to identify different withholding tax types.

3. Define Withholding Tax Type for Payment Posting – 104226

Withholding taxes can be deducted at two points in time; they can be deducted at the time of invoicing or at payment. Therefore, for this withholding tax type, one withholding tax type is created for payments and one for invoices.

4. Define Withholding Tax Type for Invoice Posting – 104225

Withholding taxes can be deducted at two points in time; they can be deducted at the time of invoicing or at payment. Therefore, for this withholding tax type, one withholding tax type is created for payments and one for invoices.

5. Assign Withholding Tax Types to Company Code – 103914

6. Define Withholding Tax Codes - 104227

In the withholding tax codes, maintain different tax rates and maintain the basis for the corresponding deductions.

7. Define Min/Max Amounts for Withholding Codes– 103915

If required, you can define a minimum/maximum limit amount for which the withholding tax code is valid, or, if no amount is specified, there is no limit to the lower and lower amount for withholding tax.

8. Assign Withholding Tax key to Withholding Tax Code – 105553

9. Assign Exchange Rate Type for Withholding Tax Type - 104400

10. Automatic Account Determination for Withholding Tax Code– 100297

11. Check Withholding Tax Configuration

①Check Company Code

②Check Business Partner

③Check Withholding Tax Type

④Simulate Calculation

 

  • Withholding Tax Demos

Withholding tax in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is also called extended withholding tax and contains withholding tax types and withholding tax codes. The withholding tax type represents the base calculation rule. The withholding tax code represents a specific feature of these rules, especially the percentage rate.

If more than one withholding tax is required for a particular transaction, this is covered in the SAP system by defining several withholding tax types. When you enter line items, you can enter withholding tax data for each withholding tax type. Note, however, that if you have two or more sets of withholding tax codes and types in an invoice, you must decide whether all the proposed withholding tax codes and types are relevant for the transaction. If you do not calculate and post some of the proposed withholding tax, you must remove the transaction-independent withholding tax code.

1. Scenario: Post Withholding Tax at Point of Invoice

1). Maintain Business Partner Master Data

From the screenshot above, you can see that the vendor withholding tax type is set as the point in time of invoice posting to generate withholding tax line items with withholding tax code T1 that is 20% withholding tax rate.

2). Post Supplier Invoice

View the financial entries generated by the simulation:

You will see that a withholding tax document entry was generated to deduct the payable amount.

2. Scenario: Post Withholding Tax at Point of Payment

1). Maintain Business Partner Master Data

From the screenshot above, you can see that the vendor withholding tax type is set as the point of payment to generate withholding tax line items with withholding tax code T1, which is 20% withholding tax rate.

2). Post Supplier Invoice

View the financial entries generated by the simulation:

3). Payment of Supplier Invoice

Simulate the payment document and you can see that at the time of payment generates the withholding tax line items in the following figure.

Currently, for the withholding tax posting point in Taiwan, customers can choose the corresponding scenario as per their requirements.

 

  • Report for Withholding Tax

For some countries/regions, the withholding tax report is generated based on the information collected from supplier and customer documents, such as cleared invoices, supplier credit memos, down payments, and cleared credit memos. The system generates a text file that can be submitted to the government authorities. Once the Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) feature is active for some countries/regions, you can use the Run Statutory Reports to generate withholding tax report.

For example, the withholding tax report for the United States is as follows:

Withholding Tax (1099-MISC) Report for the US

The withholding tax reporting information for Belgium is as follows:

Withholding Tax Report in Belgium

However, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not yet provide a standard withholding tax declaration report for Taiwan and user can use the following CDS View to get the corresponding withholding tax information:

  • I_SupplierWithHoldingTax: Supplier Withholding Tax
  • I_CustomerWithTax: Customer Withholding Tax
  • I_WithholdingTaxCode: withholding tax code
  • I_SuplrInvcHeaderWhldgTaxAPI01 : Withholding Tax on Supplier Invoice

Customers already sent requirement of withholding tax declaration reports to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition product departments and look forward to the withholding tax declaration report delivered by SAP in the future localization functionality for Taiwan. Please refer to the following link to check detail request on customer influence portal:

Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence

Starting with CE2502, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition offers a new application, Withholding Tax Calculation Analysis, which answers the question, “How do you calculate withholding tax for this transaction?”, as follows is an example of a German company code:

For information about the app, refer to the help document: Withholding Tax Calculation Analysis | SAP Help Portal

However, the app is currently not supported for Taiwan and prompts the following information:

Hope the app will be open for Taiwan in the future.

 

  • Conclusion

In the Taiwan region, withholding tax is applicable for different income types, such as dividends, rents, interest, and royalties generated internally in Taiwan. It is important to ensure timely taxation, simplify tax management, and enforce compliance.

The withholding agent shall, prior to the tenth day of each month, pay to the Treasury the taxes withheld the previous month and make out withholding certificates and submit them to the tax offices not later than the January of the following tax year, specifying the payment made and the tax withheld in the previous year. In the case that three national holidays occur in immediate succession in January the period for the submission of the withholding certificates shall be extended to February 5 .For income paid to non-residents or foreign businesses having no permanent establishment in the ROC, the withholding agent shall pay to the Treasury the taxes withheld and file a withholding report and the receipts with the tax offices within ten days after withholding.

Reference link [4]

Like many tax types in Taiwan, misunderstandings of tax declarations or missed deadlines can cause economic losses for foreign companies inside and outside Taiwan.

 

  • Reference Information
  1. Help Portal Documentation | Withholding Tax
  2. Virtual Data Model and CDS Views | Withholding Tax for Supplier Invoice
  3. Taiwan withholding Tax Guide | Tax | SongjerCPA
  4. When shall a holding agent file returns and make payments of tax withheld?
  5. Withholding Tax (1099-MISC) Report in the US
  6. Withholding Tax Report in Belgium

entry.title: Simplifying SAP Note Implementation with TCI for Electronic Documents
entry.content:
We're excited to announce the release of Transport-Based Correction Instructions (TCI) for electronic document processing, a game-changer for implementing SAP Notes. TCI streamlines the process, making it faster and more efficient.

What is TCI?

Transport-Based Correction Instructions (TCI) are pre-packaged solutions that bundle multiple SAP Notes into a single transport request. This approach eliminates the need for manual SAP Note implementation, reducing complexity and saving time.

New TCIs available for electronic documents

Electronic communication integration framework

SAP Note 3559476 - Transport based Correction Instructions (TCI) for the Electronic Communication Integration Framework

  • This TCI includes all SAP Notes published before December 31, 2024, for packages GLO_GEN_GCO_COMMUNICATION and GLO_GEN_GCO_COMMUNICATION_AIF.
  • Application component: CA-GTF-CSC-DRC-COM 
  • Supported releases:
    • S4CORE: Versions 104 to 108
    • SAP_APPL: Version 606 (SP12), 617 (SP05), and 618 (initial support package)

Electronic document processing framework

SAP Note 3545430 - Transport-Based Correction Instructions (TCI) for the Electronic Document Processing Framework

  • This TCI includes the following:
    • Framework SAP Notes up to Framework 36
    • Inbound Message Handler
    • Customer Invoice (Peppol and via E-Mail) core components
    • Supplier Invoice (Peppol and via E-Mail) core components
    • Peppol Customer Invoice (Austria, Belgium, Germany, Denmark, Netherlands, Norway, Sweden, Ireland, Poland)
    • Peppol Supplier Invoice (Received by E-Mail) for Germany
  • Supported releases:
    • S4CORE: Versions 104 to 108
    • SAP_APPL: Version 606 (SP12), 617 (SP05), and 618 (initial support package)

Why TCI matters

TCI implementation simplifies the process, ensuring that your systems are up-to-date with the latest SAP Notes without the hassle of manual updates.

Give us your feedback

To ensure that we’re covering what you need, please leave your feedback at the bottom of this blog post!

#SAPGoGlobal #SAPLocalization

entry.title: 集合貼：台灣地區常見本地化需求在SAP ERP公有雲中的實施方式建議
entry.content:
SAP ERP公有雲解決方案針對台灣地區提供了許多本地化功能，例如符合當地法規的財務報表、稅務申報、電子發票管理、資料合規處理等，完整信息可參考 Localization for Taiwan in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition。

過去幾年來，許多用戶和實施商在具體應用該系統後給予SAP大量有益的反饋。本文將聚焦其中幾個重要話題，借助架構師的見解，為台灣地區的SAP應用生態圈的客戶和實施夥伴提供參考，幫助企業更好地利用SAP ERP雲系統來推動數位化管理。

 

基於利潤中心的成本核算

利潤中心評價是企業財務管理中不可或缺的一部分，專注於評估各業務單位或部門的財務表現。透過將收入和成本歸屬於特定的利潤中心，企業能夠分析盈利能力、促進問責制及支持戰略決策制定。目前，SAP ERP公有雲暫不支援此類業務需求，参考：3371144 - Profit Center Valuation is not supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud system 、Profit Center Valuation | SAP Customer Influence Portal 。建議企業在前期溝通中了解產品現狀及設計理念，並可能考慮依諮詢商的業務優化建議調整管理方式，運用SAP提供的最佳實踐。

 

外勞採購的稅代扣代繳與申報

在台灣，透過外勞的採購來支持企業內外需求相當常見。當地法規要求企業為這類採購進行稅代扣代繳，具體可參考：Comprehensive Overview of Withholding Tax in Taiwan。目前，SAP ERP公有雲尚未提供全面的本地化解決方案，但可了解架構師提供的變通應用方案：SAP ERP公有雲台灣本土化 - 扣繳稅方案建議。

若貴司或您的企業客戶有類似需求，歡迎透過企業S-User帳號登入以下站點頁面，投下您寶貴的一票，以助SAP產品部門更好地規劃相關功能的實現：Withholding Tax Request | SAP Customer Influence Portal。

 

台灣統一發票管理及VAT申報

依台灣地區相關法規，目前（2025年）有效的統一發票形式共有五種。SAP ERP公有雲提供了電子發票的標準端到端解決方案。關於統一發票在SAP ERP公有雲中的管理建議、方案應用注意事項及與加值中心集成申報稅務的實施方式建議，請參閱：統一發票在 SAP ERP 公有雲系統中的實施方案建議。

 

LCM報表

LCM報表是依IFRS準則下LCNRV（Lower Cost or Net Realizable Value，成本與可變現淨值孰低法，參考IAS 2 Inventories）庫存評價方法的財務報表。根據台灣法規，已上市或準備IPO的企業需要定期披露LCM報表。目前，SAP ERP公有雲中的庫存評價標準APP尚未支援LCNRV方法，企業需客製化報表以滿足相關法規需求。如需此功能，請在以下頁面投票：Inventory Valuation Enhancement for IFRS Compliance with LCNRV Method | SAP Customer Influence Portal。

 

庫齡表

台灣上市企業需藉ERP系統的庫齡表有效管理庫存資產，確保合規及財務報表的準確性，改善資產流動性，減少損失，提高競爭力。SAP ERP公有雲目前提供標準應用 "Inventory KPI Analysis" 供企業分析庫存指標。企業或SAP實施夥伴亦可透過客製化報表滿足深度定製庫齡表的需求。更多相關博客可在 SAP Community 查詢。

entry.title: 集锦帖：台湾地区常见本地化需求在SAP ERP公有云中的实施方式建议
entry.content:
SAP ERP公有云解决方案针对台湾地区提供了许多本地化功能，例如符合当地法规的财务报表、税务申报、电子发票管理、数据合规处理等，完整信息可参考 S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition中针对台湾的本地化功能。

过去几年中，许多用户和实施商在应用该系统后给予SAP大量有益反馈。本文将聚焦其中几个重要话题，通过架构师的见解，为台湾地区的SAP应用生态圈的客户和实施伙伴提供参考，帮助企业更好地利用SAP ERP云系统推动数字化管理。

 

基于利润中心的成本核算

利润中心评估是企业财务管理中不可或缺的一部分，专注于评估各业务单位或部门的财务表现。通过将收入和成本归集到特定的利润中心，企业能够分析盈利能力、促进问责制及支持战略决策制定。目前，SAP ERP公有云暂不支持此类业务需求，参考：3371144 - SAP S/4HANA Cloud 系统中不支持利润中心评估、Profit Center Valuation | SAP Customer Influence Portal。建议企业在前期沟通中了解SAP产品现状及设计理念，并根据咨询商的业务优化建议调整企业管理方式，运用SAP提供的最佳实践。

 

外劳采购的税代扣代缴与申报

在中国台湾，通过外劳的采购来支持企业内外需求相当常见。当地法规要求企业为这类采购进行税代扣代缴，具体可参考：Comprehensive Overview of Withholding Tax in Taiwan。目前，SAP ERP公有云尚未提供全面的本地化解决方案，但可了解架构师提供的变通应用方案：SAP ERP公有云台湾本地化-代扣税实施建议。

若贵司或您的企业客户有类似需求，欢迎通过企业S-User账户登录以下页面，投下您宝贵的一票，以助SAP产品部门更好地规划相关功能的实现：Withholding Tax Request | SAP Customer Influence Portal。

 

台湾统一发票管理及VAT申报

根据台湾地区相关法规，目前（2025年）有效的统一发票形式共有五种。SAP ERP公有云提供了电子发票的标准端到端解决方案。关于统一发票在SAP ERP公有云中的管理建议、方案应用注意事项、及与加值中心集成申报VAT的实施方式建议，请参阅：台湾地区统一发票在 SAP ERP 公有云系统中的实施方案建议。

 

LCM报表

LCM报表是依据IFRS准则下LCNRV（成本与可变现净值孰低法，参考 IAS 2 Inventories ）库存估值方法的财务报表。根据台湾法规，已上市或准备IPO的企业需要定期披露LCM报表。目前，SAP ERP公有云中的库存估值标准APP尚未支持LCNRV方法，企业需定制报表以满足相关法规需求。如需此功能，请在以下页面投票：Inventory Valuation Enhancement for IFRS Compliance with LCNRV Method | SAP Customer Influence Portal。

 

库龄表

台湾上市企业需通过ERP系统的库龄表有效管理库存资产，确保合规及财务报表的准确性，改善资产流动性，减少损失，提高竞争力。SAP ERP公有云目前提供标准应用 "库存KPI分析" 供企业分析库存指标。企业或SAP实施伙伴亦可通过定制化报表满足深度定制库龄表的需求。更多相关博客可在 SAP Community 查询。

entry.title: Recommendations for Implementing Common Localization Requirements for Taiwan with SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition offers various localized features tailored for the Taiwan region, such as financial reporting compliant with local regulations, tax reporting, electronic invoice management, and data compliance processing. For complete information, please refer to Localization for Taiwan in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Over the past few years, many users and implementation partners have provided SAP with valuable feedback following their practical application of the system. This blog focuses on several important topics, drawing insights from SAP solution architects to offer guidance to clients and implementation partners within Taiwan's SAP ecosystem. The goal is to help businesses better utilize the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system to drive digital management.

 

Profit Center Valuation

Profit center valuation is an important part of corporate financial management in some enterprises, focusing on evaluating the financial performance of various business units or departments. By attributing revenue and costs to specific profit centers, businesses can analyze profitability, enhance accountability, and support strategic decision-making. Currently, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not support this business requirement. Refer to: 3371144 - Profit Center Valuation is not supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud system and Profit Center Valuation | SAP Customer Influence Portal. It's recommended for enterprises to understand the current SAP product status and design philosophy during preliminary discussions in the Discover phase and consider adjusting management approaches based on business optimization suggestions from consultants, using SAP's best practices.

 

Withholding and Reporting Tax for Foreign Labor Procurement

In Taiwan, it is common to procure foreign labor to support internal operation and customer sales business needs. Local regulations in Taiwan require companies to withhold and report taxes for such procurements. For details, refer to: Comprehensive Overview of Withholding Tax in Taiwan. Currently, the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not offer a comprehensive localized solution specifically for Taiwan, but alternative application solutions provided by architects can be explored: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Taiwan Localization - Workaround Solution for Withholding Tax.

If your company or corporate client has similar needs, feel free to log in with your company's S-User account to the following webpage to cast your valuable vote, thereby aiding the SAP product department in better planning the implementation of related functionalities: Withholding Tax Request | SAP Customer Influence Portal.

 

Government Uniform Invoice (GUI) Management and VAT Reporting in Taiwan

According to Taiwan's relevant regulations, there are currently (as of 2025) five valid forms of GUI. The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides a standard end-to-end solution for electronic invoicing. For recommendations on managing GUI within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, application considerations, and implementation suggestions for tax reporting integration with value-added centers, please refer to: Expert Insights on Adopting Solutions for Taiwan's Government Uniform Invoices (GUI).

 

LCM Reporting

LCM reports are financial statements based on the IFRS-standard LCNRV (Lower Cost or Net Realizable Value, according to IAS 2 Inventories) inventory valuation method. In Taiwan, companies listed or preparing for an IPO are required to regularly disclose LCM reports. Currently, the inventory valuation standard app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not support the LCNRV method, requiring companies to customize reports to fulfill related regulatory requirements. If you need this functionality, please vote on the following page: Inventory Valuation Enhancement for IFRS Compliance with LCNRV Method | SAP Customer Influence Portal.

 

Inventory Aging Report

Taiwan-listed companies need to effectively manage inventory assets using Inventory Aging Reports within ERP systems to ensure compliance and accuracy of financial statements, improve asset liquidity, reduce losses, and enhance competitiveness. The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition currently offers a standard application "Inventory KPI Analysis" for enterprises to analyze inventory metrics. Enterprises or SAP implementation partners can also fulfill the need for deeply customized Inventory Aging Reports through custom reporting. More related blogs can be found at  SAP Community .
